ddls:
CREATE TABLE "recognition_status" (
  "recognition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the recognition status entry */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the disease */,
  "recognized_by_oie" INTEGER /* Indicates if the disease is recognized by the OIE */,
  "recognized_by_eu" INTEGER /* Indicates if the disease is recognized by the EU */,
  "recognized_date" TEXT /* Date when the disease was recognized */,
  "status_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the recognition status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recognition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the disease */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* The comment text about the disease */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date when the comment was made */,
  "comment_author" TEXT /* Author of the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "risk_management_programs" (
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each risk management program */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the disease */,
  "program_details" TEXT /* Details about the risk management program */,
  "program_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the risk management program */,
  "program_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the risk management program */,
  "program_status" TEXT /* Status of the risk management program (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("program_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, veterinarian, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* ID of the disease being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the disease information */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the disease information was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "country" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disease_outbreaks" (
  "outbreak_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease outbreak */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the disease */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "outbreak_date" TEXT /* Date when the outbreak occurred */,
  "outbreak_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the outbreak */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outbreak_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_disease_outbreaks_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "risk_factors" (
  "risk_factor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each risk factor */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the disease */,
  "risk_factor" TEXT /* Name of the risk factor */,
  "risk_factor_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the risk factor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("risk_factor_id")
)
